episode_title: When AI Radio Starts To Know Your Taste
note: ffprobe was not found, so the renderer used an ffmpeg-only fallback.

segments.speaker: Host A
segments.text: Have you noticed that some AI hosts are starting to feel less random?

segments.speaker: Host B
segments.text: Yes. The good ones sound like they know what kind of day you are having.

segments.speaker: Host A
segments.text: If you listen to AI products and business technology on your commute, the next episode should not be generic tech news.

segments.speaker: Host B
segments.text: It should know you care about the product logic behind the story, not just the headline.

segments.speaker: Host A
segments.text: That is where personalization gets interesting. The voice is only the surface.

segments.speaker: Host B
segments.text: The real value is that it remembers your listening habits, your questions, and the way you like things explained.

segments.speaker: Host A
segments.text: And when it works, it feels less like a feed and more like a host who prepared for you.

segments.speaker: Host B
segments.text: That is the kind of AI radio I would actually come back to tomorrow.
